speaker: WARWICK
text: Forspent with toil, as runners with a race,

speaker: WARWICK
text: I lay me down a little while to breathe,

speaker: WARWICK
text: For strokes received and many blows repaid

speaker: WARWICK
text: Have robbed my strong-knit sinews of their strength;

speaker: WARWICK
text: And spite of spite, needs must I rest awhile.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Smile, gentle heaven, or strike, ungentle death,

speaker: EDWARD
text: For this world frowns and Edward’s sun is clouded.

speaker: WARWICK
text: How now, my lord, what hap? What hope of good?

speaker: GEORGE
text: Our hap is loss, our hope but sad despair;

speaker: GEORGE
text: Our ranks are broke, and ruin follows us.

speaker: GEORGE
text: What counsel give you? Whither shall we fly?

speaker: EDWARD
text: Bootless is flight; they follow us with wings,

speaker: EDWARD
text: And weak we are and cannot shun pursuit.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ah, Warwick, why hast thou withdrawn thyself?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thy brother’s blood the thirsty earth hath drunk,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Broached with the steely point of Clifford’s lance,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And in the very pangs of death he cried,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Like to a dismal clangor heard from far,

speaker: RICHARD
text: “Warwick, revenge! Brother, revenge my death!”

speaker: RICHARD
text: So, underneath the belly of their steeds,

speaker: RICHARD
text: That stained their fetlocks in his smoking blood,

speaker: RICHARD
text: The noble gentleman gave up the ghost.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Then let the earth be drunken with our blood!

speaker: WARWICK
text: I’ll kill my horse because I will not fly.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Why stand we like soft-hearted women here,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Wailing our losses whiles the foe doth rage,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And look upon, as if the tragedy

speaker: WARWICK
text: Were played in jest by counterfeiting actors?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Here on my knee I vow to God above

speaker: WARWICK
text: I’ll never pause again, never stand still,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Till either death hath closed these eyes of mine

speaker: WARWICK
text: Or Fortune given me measure of revenge.

speaker: EDWARD
text: O Warwick, I do bend my knee with thine,

speaker: EDWARD
text: And in this vow do chain my soul to thine

speaker: EDWARD
text: And, ere my knee rise from the Earth’s cold face,

speaker: EDWARD
text: I throw my hands, mine eyes, my heart to Thee,

speaker: EDWARD
text: Thou setter up and plucker down of kings,

speaker: EDWARD
text: Beseeching Thee, if with Thy will it stands

speaker: EDWARD
text: That to my foes this body must be prey,

speaker: EDWARD
text: Yet that Thy brazen gates of heaven may ope

speaker: EDWARD
text: And give sweet passage to my sinful soul.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Now, lords, take leave until we meet again,

speaker: EDWARD
text: Where’er it be, in heaven or in Earth.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Brother, give me thy hand.—And, gentle Warwick,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Let me embrace thee in my weary arms.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I that did never weep now melt with woe

speaker: RICHARD
text: That winter should cut off our springtime so.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Away, away! Once more, sweet lords, farewell.

speaker: GEORGE
text: Yet let us all together to our troops

speaker: GEORGE
text: And give them leave to fly that will not stay,

speaker: GEORGE
text: And call them pillars that will stand to us;

speaker: GEORGE
text: And, if we thrive, promise them such rewards

speaker: GEORGE
text: As victors wear at the Olympian Games.

speaker: GEORGE
text: This may plant courage in their quailing breasts,

speaker: GEORGE
text: For yet is hope of life and victory.

speaker: GEORGE
text: Forslow no longer; make we hence amain.
